groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Campaign subject, sender, objective, audience, confirmed details, recipient relationship, CTA destination, language, tone, and variant count.

groups.title: Campaign Setup
groups.description: Campaign format, offer terms, important dates, timing context, personalization fields, and sequence spacing.

groups.title: Brand & Message
groups.description: Brand voice, required wording, restrictions, and emoji preferences for concise SMS copy.

groups.title: Compliance & Delivery
groups.description: Recipient region, opt-out handling, custom opt-out wording, and message-length target.

variables.label: Campaign subject
variables.placeholder: Summer sale, appointment reminder, event invitation, service update
variables.hint: Describe what the SMS campaign is about.

help.what: Provide the campaign subject before generating the SMS campaign.
help.why: It establishes the central subject that every message must communicate.
help.example: Good example: Appointment reminder for confirmed patients
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported urgency, fake scarcity, fake personalization, or changing the campaign into a different subject.

variables.label: Sender name
variables.placeholder: Northside Dental, Acme Store, BrightPath
variables.hint: Enter the business, organization, product, or service recipients should recognize.

help.what: Provide the sender name before generating the SMS campaign.
help.why: SMS recipients need clear sender context, especially when the sender ID may be unfamiliar.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported urgency, fake scarcity, fake personalization, or changing the campaign into a different subject.

variables.label: Campaign objective
variables.placeholder: Select the main objective
variables.hint: Choose the single most important result this campaign should achieve.

help.what: Provide the campaign objective before generating the SMS campaign.
help.why: The objective determines the message structure, CTA, urgency level, and sequence logic.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported urgency, fake scarcity, fake personalization, or changing the campaign into a different subject.

variables.label: Target audience
variables.placeholder: Existing customers who purchased in the past six months
variables.hint: Describe the recipients and their relevant relationship with the sender.

help.what: Provide the target audience before generating the SMS campaign.
help.why: Audience context materially changes relevance, vocabulary, assumptions, and CTA framing.
help.example: Good example: Existing customers who booked a consultation and agreed to receive SMS reminders.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported urgency, fake scarcity, fake personalization, or changing the campaign into a different subject.

variables.label: Essential message details
variables.placeholder: What happened, what is available, what recipients need to know, and what action matters
variables.hint: Include only confirmed facts that must appear in the messages.

help.what: Provide the essential message details before generating the SMS campaign.
help.why: The generator cannot safely infer campaign facts, terms, instructions, or product details.
help.example: Good example: The appointment is tomorrow at 10:00 AM. Recipients can reschedule from the supplied link.
help.avoid: Avoid invented prices, discounts, deadlines, product claims, availability, results, or eligibility rules.

variables.label: Recipient relationship
variables.placeholder: Select how recipients are eligible
variables.hint: Choose the closest description of why these recipients may receive the message.

help.what: Provide the recipient relationship before generating the SMS campaign.
help.why: The recipient relationship affects promotional wording, identification, opt-out handling, and compliance warnings.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported urgency, fake scarcity, fake personalization, or changing the campaign into a different subject.

variables.label: Destination URL
variables.placeholder: https://www.creatornivo.com/offer
variables.hint: Add the final destination for the primary action. Leave blank when no link is needed.

help.what: Optional context for the destination url field.
help.why: A supplied URL enables a specific, actionable CTA without inventing a destination.
help.avoid: Avoid invented, shortened, unapproved, or misleading links.

variables.label: Output language
variables.placeholder: English, Ukrainian, German, Spanish
variables.hint: Enter the language for the generated campaign.

help.what: Optional context for the output language field.
help.why: Language affects tone, encoding, character capacity, punctuation, and message length.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, manipulative pressure, unsupported claims, or private recipient data.

variables.label: Tone
variables.placeholder: Select a tone
variables.hint: Choose the overall voice without changing the confirmed facts.

help.what: Optional context for the tone field.
help.why: Tone is a genuine brand preference that materially changes the message.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, manipulative pressure, unsupported claims, or private recipient data.

variables.label: Number of variants
variables.hint: Generate between one and five distinct versions for each campaign message.

help.what: Optional context for the number of variants field.
help.why: It lets users choose between one finished message and several testable alternatives.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, manipulative pressure, unsupported claims, or private recipient data.

variables.label: Campaign format
variables.placeholder: Select campaign format
variables.hint: Choose one SMS or a short sequence with a clear progression.

help.what: Optional context for the campaign format field.
help.why: A sequence requires different pacing, repetition control, and timing recommendations.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, manipulative pressure, unsupported claims, or private recipient data.

variables.label: Offer or incentive details
variables.placeholder: Discount, eligibility, exclusions, redemption method, price, or availability
variables.hint: Provide confirmed offer terms. Leave blank for non-promotional campaigns.

help.what: Optional context for the offer or incentive details field.
help.why: Offers cannot be accurately described without supplied terms, restrictions, and eligibility details.
help.example: Good example: 20% discount applies to annual plans through the confirmed campaign date.
help.avoid: Avoid invented prices, discounts, deadlines, product claims, availability, results, or eligibility rules.

variables.label: Important date
variables.hint: Add a confirmed event, appointment, launch, or expiration date.

help.what: Optional context for the important date field.
help.why: Dates materially affect clarity and urgency and must never be invented.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, manipulative pressure, unsupported claims, or private recipient data.

variables.label: Timing context
variables.placeholder: Send one day before the appointment or during local business hours
variables.hint: Describe when the message is relevant or when it should be sent.

help.what: Optional context for the timing context field.
help.why: SMS effectiveness and appropriateness often depend on when the recipient receives it.
help.example: Good example: Send one day before the appointment during local business hours.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, manipulative pressure, unsupported claims, or private recipient data.

variables.label: Available personalization
variables.placeholder: First name, appointment time, store location, order number
variables.hint: List only data fields the sending platform can reliably insert.

help.what: Optional context for the available personalization field.
help.why: The prompt must not invent names, purchase history, locations, or other personal information.
help.example: Good example: First name, appointment time, and store location.
help.avoid: Avoid personal data that the sending platform cannot reliably insert or that recipients did not expect to be used.

variables.label: Sequence spacing
variables.placeholder: 24 hours, three days, morning of the event
variables.hint: Describe the preferred delay between messages or leave as Auto.

help.what: Optional context for the sequence spacing field.
help.why: Multi-message campaigns need an explicit or safely inferred cadence.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, manipulative pressure, unsupported claims, or private recipient data.

variables.label: Brand voice notes
variables.placeholder: Direct and practical, calm healthcare tone, casual local-business voice
variables.hint: Add distinctive voice guidance that is not already covered by the tone setting.

help.what: Optional context for the brand voice notes field.
help.why: It preserves meaningful brand characteristics without creating several overlapping style fields.
help.example: Good example: Helpful, direct, and low-pressure.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, manipulative pressure, unsupported claims, or private recipient data.

variables.label: Required wording
variables.placeholder: Required disclaimer, legal phrase, product name, booking instruction
variables.hint: Enter exact wording that must appear when space and context allow.

help.what: Optional context for the required wording field.
help.why: Some campaigns require exact names, instructions, disclosures, or approved phrases.
help.example: Good example: Reply STOP to opt out
help.avoid: Avoid vague legal instructions; add exact wording or exact restrictions when they matter.

variables.label: Restrictions and claims to avoid
variables.placeholder: Do not mention price, avoid medical promises, do not use the word free
variables.hint: List campaign-specific restrictions, prohibited claims, or sensitive wording.

help.what: Optional context for the restrictions and claims to avoid field.
help.why: The sender may have legal, brand, contractual, or factual restrictions the model cannot infer.
help.example: Good example: Do not mention price unless it is supplied.
help.avoid: Avoid vague legal instructions; add exact wording or exact restrictions when they matter.

variables.label: Emoji use
variables.placeholder: Select emoji preference
variables.hint: Remember that some symbols may affect SMS encoding and message length.

help.what: Optional context for the emoji use field.
help.why: Emoji preferences affect tone, encoding, character capacity, and deliverability.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, manipulative pressure, unsupported claims, or private recipient data.

variables.label: Recipient region
variables.placeholder: United States, United Kingdom, EU, Ukraine, Canada
variables.hint: Add the main recipient country or jurisdiction when compliance requirements may differ.

help.what: Optional context for the recipient region field.
help.why: Consent, sender identification, opt-out, and marketing-message rules can vary by jurisdiction.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, manipulative pressure, unsupported claims, or private recipient data.

variables.label: Opt-out handling
variables.placeholder: Select opt-out handling
variables.hint: Choose how unsubscribe wording should be handled in promotional messages.

help.what: Optional context for the opt-out handling field.
help.why: Opt-out wording depends on campaign type, region, recipient basis, and the sender’s existing process.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, manipulative pressure, unsupported claims, or private recipient data.

variables.label: Custom opt-out wording
variables.placeholder: Reply STOP to opt out
variables.hint: Enter the exact supported instruction used by your messaging provider.

help.what: Optional context for the custom opt-out wording field.
help.why: The generator must not invent an unsupported unsubscribe command.
help.example: Good example: Reply STOP to opt out
help.avoid: Avoid unsubscribe wording that your SMS provider does not actually support.

variables.label: Message length target
variables.placeholder: Select a length target
variables.hint: Choose a conservative single-message target or allow concise multipart delivery.

help.what: Optional context for the message length target field.
help.why: The preferred delivery format affects message compression, CTA wording, and sequence design.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, manipulative pressure, unsupported claims, or private recipient data.
